title: Wrap repr(value) and error formatting in defensive try/catch
description: EDGE-001/EDGE-002: repr(value) at src/middleware/core.jl:74 and showerror() in error formatting can themselves throw (e.g., broken show() method, type with no repr). This kills the eval handler with no error response to the client. Fix: wrap repr(value) in try/catch with fallback to string(typeof(value)) or a safe '<repr failed>' placeholder. Wrap showerror() similarly. Files: src/middleware/core.jl:74 (repr call), src/middleware/core.jl:80-85 (error formatting). Done when: a test evals an expression whose result has a broken show() method, and the response contains a fallback repr string instead of crashing.

title: Use Meta.parseall for multi-expression eval
description: CORR-002: Meta.parse at src/middleware/core.jl:54 only handles the first expression in multi-line code. Input like 'x = 1\ny = x + 1' silently drops everything after the first expression. Fix: replace Meta.parse(code) with Meta.parseall(code) and eval all expressions, returning the result of the last one (matching REPL semantics). Files: src/middleware/core.jl:54 (parse call). Done when: a test sends 'x = 1\ny = x + 1' and receives y=2 as the result, confirming both expressions were evaluated.

title: Add try/catch in handle_client! for connection-level error recovery
description: CORR-004: handle_client! at src/transport/tcp.jl:31 has no try/catch around handler(msg). Any unexpected throw kills the client connection silently with no error response. Fix: wrap the handler(msg) call in try/catch that returns a JSON-RPC error response to the client instead of dropping the connection. Files: src/transport/tcp.jl:31 (handle_client!), src/errors.jl (error response formatting). Done when: a test sends a request that causes an internal throw, and the client receives a proper JSON-RPC error response instead of a disconnection.

title: Replace process-global redirect_stdout/stderr with per-task capture
description: CORR-001: EvalMiddleware at src/middleware/core.jl:49-58 uses process-global redirect_stdout/redirect_stderr. Concurrent evals corrupt each other's captured output. Fix: Replace redirect_stdout/stderr with IOCapture.jl (add as dependency). IOCapture.capture() returns (value, output) per-eval without touching global state. Files: src/middleware/core.jl:49-58 (redirect logic), Project.toml (add IOCapture dep). Done when: two concurrent eval requests each capture only their own stdout, verified by a test that evals 'println(x)' in parallel and asserts outputs don't cross-contaminate.

title: Add ReentrantLock to SessionManager for thread safety
description: CORR-006: SessionManager at src/session/manager.jl has no synchronization. Concurrent create/close/lookup operations can corrupt the sessions dictionaries. Fix: add a ReentrantLock field to SessionManager and wrap all mutation operations (create_named_session!, destroy_named_session!, create_session!, close_session!) in lock() blocks. Read operations (lookup, list, session_count) should also lock for consistency. Cross-ref: overlaps with REPLy_jl-cfr (lifecycle race coverage) — this ticket adds the lock, cfr adds the comprehensive race condition tests. Files: src/session/manager.jl (all mutation functions). Done when: concurrent create/destroy calls from multiple tasks do not corrupt state, verified by a test spawning 10 tasks that simultaneously create and destroy sessions.
close_reason: Added ReentrantLock to SessionManager; all mutations and reads wrapped; clone_named_session! holds lock only for dict operations; concurrency test added

title: Document and validate ignored fields in mcp_eval_request
description: CORR-005: mcp_eval_request at src/mcp_adapter.jl:96 accepts fields (timeout, module) that are silently ignored. Fix: add validation that returns a 'not yet supported' error if these fields are provided, rather than silently ignoring them. This prevents user confusion without removing the fields from the schema. Cross-ref: overlaps with REPLy_jl-1le.1 which will implement these fields — at that point, remove the validation guard. Files: src/mcp_adapter.jl:96-120 (request handler). Done when: a test sends a request with a timeout field and receives a clear error saying the field is not yet supported.
close_reason: Added not-yet-supported guards for module and timeout_ms in mcp_eval_request; removed pass-through, updated tests

title: Add stub responses for unimplemented MCP tools
description: DRAFT-004: MCP adapter (src/mcp_adapter.jl:17-86) advertises julia_complete, julia_lookup, julia_load_file, and julia_interrupt which have no middleware implementation yet. Rather than removing (they have planned implementation tickets: 95q.1-3, 0gk.1), add stub handlers that return a structured 'not yet implemented' error response. Done when: calling each unimplemented tool returns a proper JSON-RPC error with code and message rather than falling through to UnknownOpMiddleware.
close_reason: Added mcp_stub_result() function returning not-yet-implemented error for julia_complete/lookup/load_file/interrupt; test added to mcp_adapter_test.jl

title: Handle Module type in clone_named_session! deepcopy
description: CORR-003: deepcopy at src/session/manager.jl:129 throws on Module-typed values. Modules are mutable so the ismutable(val) check passes, causing deepcopy to be called on them. Fix: add 'val isa Module && continue' before the deepcopy call at manager.jl:129. Done when: test creates a named session that imports a module (e.g. Base), clones it via clone_named_session!, and verifies the clone has the module binding without throwing.
close_reason: Added val isa Module && continue guard at manager.jl:129; test added to session_registry_test.jl

title: Fix tracer-bullet output capture deadlock and align output contract
description:
## Context
Rule-of-5 review found a confirmed deadlock in eval output capture for large stdout/stderr, plus a contract mismatch between docs/tests saying "streaming/chunks" and the implementation aggregating buffered output after eval completes.

## Files
- src/middleware/core.jl
- test/unit/eval_middleware_test.jl
- test/integration/pipeline_test.jl
- .wai/projects/tracer-bullet/designs/2026-04-17-testing-validation-strategy-principle-o.md
- .wai/projects/tracer-bullet/research/2026-04-17-tracer-bullet-scope-a-tracer-bullet-cuts-throu.md

## Done
- [ ] Eval output capture cannot deadlock on large stdout/stderr.
- [ ] Tests cover large-output completion and the chosen output-order contract.
- [ ] Docs/tests/code agree on buffered-vs-streaming semantics.
- [ ] just --justfile justfile test passes.

## Workflow Mandate
- Add failing tests first.
- Keep behavior focused on the tracer-bullet contract; avoid unrelated transport/session changes.

title: Expand integration pipeline coverage for tracer-bullet contract
description:
## Context
Plan section: integration-layer expectations and coverage rows for stdout ordering, unknown op, parse error, eval error, and ephemeral cleanup in `.wai/projects/tracer-bullet/designs/2026-04-17-testing-validation-strategy-principle-o.md`.

The current integration test exercises only the happy path, but the tracer-bullet design expects integration coverage for stdout ordering, unknown op, parse error, eval error, and ephemeral-session cleanup.

This task depends on streamed output support landing first so the integration assertions can reflect the intended contract.

## Files
- test/integration/pipeline_test.jl
- test/helpers/conformance.jl
- src/middleware/core.jl
- src/session/

## Done
- [ ] Integration tests cover success with streamed output, unknown op, parse error, eval error, and ephemeral-session leak detection.
- [ ] Tests assert protocol conformance and session cleanup explicitly.
- [ ] Tests assert `session_count(manager) == 0` before and after ephemeral eval flows.
- [ ] Scenario assertions cover expected value/error shape and status flags where applicable.
- [ ] `just --justfile justfile test` passes.

## Workflow Mandate
- Add failing integration tests first.
- Reuse existing pipeline/session APIs rather than adding new abstractions unless strictly required.
- This issue owns cross-middleware integration behavior and leak detection, not unit-level output chunk semantics.

title: Implement streamed stdout/stderr messages for eval responses
description:
## Context
Plan section: stdout/stderr capture, streaming-order expectations, and eval middleware coverage in `.wai/projects/tracer-bullet/designs/2026-04-17-testing-validation-strategy-principle-o.md`, plus tracer-bullet scope item `Core-operations — Core.eval in session module, stdout/stderr capture, value repr` in `.wai/projects/tracer-bullet/research/2026-04-17-tracer-bullet-scope-a-tracer-bullet-cuts-throu.md`.

The tracer-bullet implementation currently returns only value+done from eval requests, but the documented thin slice expects streamed stdout/stderr chunks before the terminal value.

## Files
- src/middleware/core.jl
- src/errors.jl
- test/unit/eval_middleware_test.jl
- test/runtests.jl
- test/helpers/conformance.jl

## Done
- [ ] Eval requests emit stdout/stderr chunks before value and done when code writes to those streams.
- [ ] Empty-code and error-path behavior remains shape-compatible with the existing protocol.
- [ ] A dedicated eval middleware unit test file covers stdout capture, stderr capture, empty code, streaming order, and err-field disambiguation.
- [ ] `just --justfile justfile test` passes.

## Workflow Mandate
- First red step: create `test/unit/eval_middleware_test.jl` and include it from `test/runtests.jl`.
- Start from failing unit tests for eval middleware.
- Keep scope limited to the tracer-bullet eval path; do not add timeouts, stdin, or new operations.
- This issue owns unit-level output chunk semantics and ordering.

title: Fix handler validation and eval session ownership
description:
## Context
Rule-of-5 review of the new tracer-bullet middleware found two correctness bugs in the freshly landed code.

`build_handler` dispatches requests directly into middleware without calling `validate_request`, so malformed requests are misclassified as `unknown-op`. Also, `EvalMiddleware` creates a fallback ephemeral session when `ctx.session` is `nothing` but never destroys it, which leaks sessions for custom middleware stacks.

## Done
- [ ] `build_handler` validates requests before middleware dispatch and preserves protocol error shapes for malformed input.
- [ ] Eval session ownership is explicit and leak-free for both the default stack and custom middleware stacks.
- [ ] Regression tests cover malformed requests through `build_handler` and session-count stability for custom eval stacks.
- [ ] `just test` passes.

## Notes
This is a bounded bug-fix pass on the newly added tracer-bullet middleware code, not a broad refactor.

title: fix: find_first_paragraph leaks code-fence content into handoff snippet
description: find_first_paragraph() in prime.rs does not track code-fence state. When handoff sections are empty (HTML comment placeholders only), it falls through to the Context section and picks up git-status lines from inside a triple-backtick block. Reproducer: wai prime in REPLy.jl shows RESUMING with 'M  .beads/issues.jsonl' instead of 'no summary yet'. Fix: toggle in_code_block on backtick-fence lines and skip their content.

title: Wire TCP transport for tracer-bullet acceptance path
description:
## Context
Plan section: TDD progression step 10 and tracer-bullet scope item "Target: eval over TCP" from `.wai/projects/tracer-bullet/research/2026-04-17-tracer-bullet-scope-a-tracer-bullet-cuts-throu.md`.

Implement the TCP listener/server lifecycle so a real socket client can send a newline-delimited `eval` request and receive the expected value+done stream.

## Files
- `src/transport/tcp.jl`
- `src/server.jl`
- `src/REPLy.jl`
- `test/helpers/tcp_client.jl`
- `test/helpers/server.jl`
- `test/e2e/eval_test.jl`

## Done
- [ ] `test/e2e/eval_test.jl` passes for the definitive acceptance test: send `{"op":"eval","id":"1","code":"1+1"}` over TCP and receive `value` then `done`.
- [ ] The server can bind with `port=0` and exposes the assigned port to tests.
- [ ] Shutdown happens reliably in `finally` paths so tests do not leak sockets/tasks.
- [ ] `just test` passes for unit + integration + the main e2e acceptance path.

## Workflow Mandate
- Begin with the failing end-to-end acceptance test.
- Keep the transport limited to TCP; Unix sockets and other listeners remain deferred.
- Avoid speculative abstraction beyond what the tracer-bullet path exercises.
notes: Implemented a minimal TCP tracer-bullet server: serve(port=0) binds an OS-assigned port, spawns an accept loop plus per-client tasks, routes newline-delimited JSON requests through build_handler, and closes listener/client sockets in finally-safe shutdown paths. just test is now fully green, including the e2e eval-over-TCP acceptance path.

title: Harden tracer bullet for concurrent clients and disconnects
description:
## Context
Plan section: TDD progression steps 11-12 and the e2e hardening scenarios in `.wai/projects/tracer-bullet/designs/2026-04-17-testing-validation-strategy-principle-o.md`.

Extend the tracer bullet to survive two concurrent clients and client disconnects mid-eval without violating protocol invariants or leaking server resources.

## Files
- `src/transport/tcp.jl`
- `src/server.jl`
- `src/session/`
- `test/e2e/eval_test.jl`
- `test/helpers/server.jl`
- `test/helpers/tcp_client.jl`

## Done
- [ ] `test/e2e/eval_test.jl` covers two concurrent connections and client disconnect mid-eval.
- [ ] The server continues serving other clients after one client disconnects unexpectedly.
- [ ] No resource leaks or double-done responses appear in the new e2e scenarios.
- [ ] `just test` passes for the full tracer-bullet suite.

## Workflow Mandate
- Add the failing e2e hardening tests before changing production code.
- Keep the work focused on robustness of the existing thin slice; do not expand scope to limits, security, or new operations.
- If cleanup falls out of the fix, keep it isolated or move it to the tidy ticket.
notes: Added e2e hardening coverage for two concurrent TCP clients and for a client that disconnects mid-eval. The server now tolerates write-side IO failures in per-client tasks, keeps serving healthy clients, and just test remains green for the full tracer-bullet suite.

title: Implement eval middleware streaming for tracer bullet
description:
## Context
Plan section: TDD progression step 5 and eval/error streaming coverage for `REQ-RPL-011`, `REQ-RPL-011b`, and the stdout/stderr ordering constraints described in the tracer-bullet design.

Implement the eval middleware that executes code in a session module, captures stdout/stderr, emits value messages, and distinguishes stderr chunks from error responses.

## Files
- `src/middleware/eval.jl`
- `src/eval/runtime.jl`
- `src/REPLy.jl`
- `test/unit/eval_middleware_test.jl`
- `test/helpers/conformance.jl`

## Done
- [ ] `test/unit/eval_middleware_test.jl` covers stdout capture, stderr capture, value repr, empty code no-op behavior, streaming order, and err-field disambiguation.
- [ ] Production code evaluates against the provided session module and emits a done-terminated response stream.
- [ ] Runtime errors are left shape-compatible with the shared error response contract.
- [ ] `just test` passes with the new eval middleware unit tests included in the suite.

## Workflow Mandate
- Start with failing eval middleware tests.
- Keep success-path green work separate from broader refactors.
- Do not add deferred features such as timeout enforcement, stdin, or silent/store-history handling.
notes: Verified current implementation in src/middleware/core.jl satisfies the eval streaming slice for the tracer bullet: eval requests are intercepted, empty code returns repr(nothing), runtime and parse errors flow through eval_error_response, fallback ephemeral sessions are cleaned up, and just test passes with unit/error/integration coverage.

title: Implement minimal middleware chain and error responders
description:
## Context
Plan section: TDD progression steps 6-7 plus coverage for `REQ-RPL-050`, `REQ-RPL-061`, and `REQ-RPL-063`.

Implement minimal middleware composition plus unknown-op and error response behavior so the tracer bullet can route `eval`, pass through unhandled ops, and emit protocol-conformant failures.

## Files
- `src/middleware/core.jl`
- `src/middleware/session.jl`
- `src/middleware/unknown_op.jl`
- `src/errors.jl`
- `src/REPLy.jl`
- `test/unit/middleware_test.jl`
- `test/unit/error_test.jl`

## Done
- [ ] `test/unit/middleware_test.jl` covers pass-through for unhandled ops and interception for handled ops.
- [ ] `test/unit/error_test.jl` covers eval runtime error shape, parse error shape, unknown operation shape, status flags, and no-double-done behavior.
- [ ] Production code provides the minimal middleware chain: `SessionMiddleware -> EvalMiddleware -> UnknownOpMiddleware`.
- [ ] `just test` passes with the new middleware and error unit tests included in the suite.

## Workflow Mandate
- Write failing middleware/error tests first.
- Keep the middleware subset intentionally minimal; deferred middlewares stay out of scope.
- Separate cleanup/generalization from the green implementation step.

title: Assemble tracer-bullet pipeline and pass integration tests
description:
## Context
Plan section: TDD progression steps 8-9 and the integration-layer expectations in `.wai/projects/tracer-bullet/designs/2026-04-17-testing-validation-strategy-principle-o.md`.

Assemble the handler context and middleware pipeline so request Dicts can flow through the tracer-bullet stack without network transport.

## Files
- `src/pipeline.jl`
- `src/REPLy.jl`
- `test/integration/pipeline_test.jl`
- `test/helpers/conformance.jl`

## Done
- [ ] `test/integration/pipeline_test.jl` passes for success, stdout ordering, unknown op, eval error, parse error, and ephemeral-session leak detection.
- [ ] Production code exposes a minimal pipeline assembly API used by higher-level transport code.
- [ ] Shared conformance assertions run cleanly against integration responses.
- [ ] `just test` passes for unit + integration layers.

## Workflow Mandate
- Start from the existing failing integration tests and only add implementation needed to satisfy them.
- Reuse the unit-tested components rather than re-encoding logic in the pipeline layer.
- Hold refactors for the tidy ticket unless they are strictly required to complete the assembly.
notes: Verified build_handler assembles the minimal Session→Eval→UnknownOp stack, test/integration/pipeline_test.jl passes through shared conformance assertions, and just test is green for unit+integration layers.

title: Implement message framing and protocol boundary checks
description:
## Context
Plan section: TDD progression step 3 plus protocol coverage for `REQ-RPL-001`, `REQ-RPL-001b`, `REQ-RPL-004`, `REQ-RPL-005`, `REQ-RPL-007`, `REQ-RPL-008`, and `REQ-RPL-040b`.

Implement newline-delimited JSON parsing/serialization and boundary checks so requests/responses obey the tracer-bullet wire contract.

## Files
- `src/protocol/message.jl`
- `src/REPLy.jl`
- `test/unit/message_test.jl`
- `test/helpers/conformance.jl`

## Done
- [ ] `test/unit/message_test.jl` covers parse/serialize, newline framing, malformed JSON, blank lines, partial reads, non-object JSON, disconnect handling, and overlong id rejection.
- [ ] Production code parses only flat JSON objects and serializes newline-terminated responses.
- [ ] Response helpers preserve request id echo and kebab-case keys needed by conformance checks.
- [ ] `just test` passes with the new message-focused unit tests included in the suite.

## Workflow Mandate
- Write the failing message/protocol tests first.
- Make the smallest implementation that turns the targeted unit tests green.
- Avoid bundling unrelated session or transport behavior into this step.

title: Implement ephemeral session manager for tracer bullet
description:
## Context
Plan section: TDD progression step 4 and session coverage for `REQ-RPL-030` and `REQ-RPL-035`.

Implement ephemeral light-session support backed by anonymous `Module`s so eval requests without a session can run in an isolated context and clean up afterward.

## Files
- `src/session/manager.jl`
- `src/session/module_session.jl`
- `src/REPLy.jl`
- `test/unit/session_test.jl`
- `test/integration/pipeline_test.jl`

## Done
- [ ] `test/unit/session_test.jl` covers anonymous module creation, binding isolation, cleanup, and `session_count` leak detection.
- [ ] Production code exposes a session manager API sufficient for ephemeral session creation and teardown.
- [ ] The implementation supports the integration test expectation that session count returns to zero after an ephemeral eval completes.
- [ ] `just test` passes with the new session-focused unit tests included in the suite.

## Workflow Mandate
- Drive the design from failing session tests first.
- Keep the implementation limited to ephemeral sessions; persistent/clone/close flows are out of scope.
- Defer structural cleanup or extra abstractions to the tidy ticket unless they are required to go green.

title: Scaffold tracer-bullet test harness and shared helpers
description:
## Context
Plan section: TDD progression steps 1-2 and the shared helper design in `.wai/projects/tracer-bullet/designs/2026-04-17-testing-validation-strategy-principle-o.md`.

Scaffold the acceptance/integration test targets and shared helper files before core implementation so the tracer bullet has a concrete red test shape.

## Files
- `test/runtests.jl`
- `test/helpers/conformance.jl`
- `test/helpers/tcp_client.jl`
- `test/helpers/server.jl`
- `test/integration/pipeline_test.jl`
- `test/e2e/eval_test.jl`
- `src/REPLy.jl`

## Expected Helper APIs
- `test/helpers/conformance.jl`: `assert_conformance(msgs::Vector{Dict}, request_id::String)`
- `test/helpers/tcp_client.jl`: `collect_until_done(sock)::Vector{Dict}` plus the minimal request-send helper needed by e2e tests
- `test/helpers/server.jl`: `with_server(f; port=0)`

## Done
- [ ] `test/runtests.jl` includes helpers → unit → integration → e2e in the planned order.
- [ ] Shared helper files exist with minimal public APIs/stubs aligned to the design doc.
- [ ] `test/integration/pipeline_test.jl` and `test/e2e/eval_test.jl` contain the initial failing tracer-bullet cases.
- [ ] The suite fails for missing implementation reasons, not missing include/file errors.

## Workflow Mandate
- Start red: write failing tests/helpers before adding production code.
- Keep scaffolding minimal; do not implement transport/pipeline behavior here.
- Any cleanup beyond minimal setup belongs in the later tidy ticket.

title: Implement tracer bullet: eval over TCP thin slice
description:
## Context
Implement the tracer bullet defined in `.wai/projects/tracer-bullet/research/2026-04-17-tracer-bullet-scope-a-tracer-bullet-cuts-throu.md` and the outside-in TDD sequence in `.wai/projects/tracer-bullet/designs/2026-04-17-testing-validation-strategy-principle-o.md`.

This epic tracks the thinnest end-to-end slice: `eval` over TCP with newline-delimited JSON, ephemeral sessions, the minimal middleware chain, and done-terminated streaming responses.

## Files
- `src/REPLy.jl`
- `src/protocol/`
- `src/session/`
- `src/middleware/`
- `src/transport/`
- `test/helpers/`
- `test/unit/`
- `test/integration/`
- `test/e2e/`
- `test/runtests.jl`

## Done
- [ ] The tracer-bullet child issues are completed in dependency order.
- [ ] `just test` passes with the new end-to-end, integration, and unit coverage.
- [ ] The thin slice satisfies the target TCP eval exchange and shared protocol conformance checks.

## Workflow Mandate
- Follow outside-in TDD: acceptance tests first, then integration, then unit-driven implementation.
- Apply Tidy First: keep cleanup/refactor work separate from feature-green steps.
- Prefer small, reviewable commits and keep issue notes updated with design decisions.

title: Truncate eval repr output beyond a configurable threshold
description: EDGE-003: Eval responses currently call repr(value) without an output-size cap, so printing a very large array/string can allocate unbounded memory and produce oversized replies. Add a configurable repr/output threshold in the eval path and truncate output beyond that threshold with an explicit marker. Files: src/middleware/core.jl:74-85 (value formatting and response construction), relevant eval middleware tests. Chosen approach: cap the serialized output returned to the client and include a visible truncation marker instead of attempting full rendering.
acceptance_criteria: Done when: (1) evaluating an expression with a huge display representation returns a truncated string with a marker, (2) normal small values are unaffected, and (3) regression tests cover both cases.

title: Validate named session names at middleware boundaries
description: EDGE-003: Named-session operations currently reject only empty names, but do not validate length, format, or disallowed characters. Add explicit validation for session names at the request boundary for session operations and any other externally supplied named-session entry points. Files: src/middleware/session_ops.jl:41-87 (close-session / clone-session parameter validation), src/middleware/session.jl or other request-routing entry points that accept named-session identifiers, plus session operation tests. Chosen approach: centralize validation in a helper used by the middleware boundary so invalid names are rejected before registry lookup or mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Done when: (1) invalid names such as empty/whitespace-only or names containing disallowed characters are rejected with a clear error, (2) valid names continue to work, and (3) tests cover both acceptance and rejection cases.

title: Limit inbound TCP message size and reject oversized requests
description: EDGE-003: The TCP transport currently accepts arbitrarily large JSON messages, which can exhaust memory before request handling begins. Implement a configurable maximum message size at the transport boundary and reject oversized requests with a structured JSON-RPC error while keeping the server process healthy. Files: src/transport/tcp.jl (message read loop / request intake), test/e2e or test/integration transport coverage. Chosen approach: enforce the limit while reading from the socket rather than after full buffering, so the server never materializes an unbounded payload in memory.
acceptance_criteria: Done when: (1) a request just under the limit is processed normally, (2) a request over the limit returns a clear error response or clean disconnect per protocol choice, and (3) a regression test proves the limit is enforced in src/transport/tcp.jl.

title: Add timeout to collect_reply_stream and close_server!
description: EDGE-003/EDGE-004: collect_reply_stream at src/mcp_adapter.jl:138 and close_server! at src/transport/tcp.jl have no timeouts. A stuck eval or unresponsive connection blocks indefinitely. Fix: add configurable timeout (default 30s) to collect_reply_stream using Timer/timedwait. Add a timeout to close_server! that force-closes after grace period. Cross-ref: overlaps with REPLy_jl-v3f which covers broader timeout/disconnect cleanup — this ticket adds the basic timeout mechanism, v3f adds comprehensive disconnect handling. Files: src/mcp_adapter.jl:138 (collect_reply_stream), src/transport/tcp.jl (close_server!). Done when: a test verifies that a stuck eval times out after the configured period and returns a timeout error response.
close_reason: collect_reply_stream timeout (timedwait+@async, task-local collected, try/catch on close), close_server! grace_seconds, both validated, Ro5 findings fixed

title: Boundary hardening umbrella: message size, repr truncation, and session name validation
description:
Umbrella for three independent boundary-hardening fixes identified in Ro5 review. The original bundle has been split into child tickets so transport limits, eval output limits, and session-name validation can be implemented and tested independently. Child tickets:
- REPLy_jl-npb.1: inbound TCP message size limit in src/transport/tcp.jl
- REPLy_jl-npb.2: repr truncation in src/middleware/core.jl
- REPLy_jl-npb.3: named-session validation at middleware boundaries

Rationale for split: these concerns touch different subsystems, have different test shapes, and do not need to land together.

title: Add docstrings to serve(), RequestContext, HandlerContext, dispatch_middleware
description: CLAR-001: Key public API functions lack docstrings. Fix: add Julia docstrings to: serve() in src/transport/tcp.jl, RequestContext/HandlerContext in src/types.jl, dispatch_middleware in src/middleware/pipeline.jl. Files: src/transport/tcp.jl, src/types.jl, src/middleware/pipeline.jl. Done when: help('serve'), help('RequestContext'), help('dispatch_middleware') in Julia REPL show meaningful documentation.

title: Extract EvalMiddleware to its own file
description: DRAFT-001: EvalMiddleware is defined inside src/middleware/core.jl alongside CoreMiddleware. It should be in its own file for clarity and to match the one-middleware-per-file convention used by other middleware (session.jl, unknown.jl). Fix: extract EvalMiddleware and its eval logic to src/middleware/eval.jl, add include() in src/REPLy.jl. Files: src/middleware/core.jl (source), src/middleware/eval.jl (target, new file), src/REPLy.jl (include). Done when: EvalMiddleware lives in its own file, all tests pass, and core.jl only contains CoreMiddleware.

title: Add idle sweep support and lifecycle race coverage
description: Extend src/session/manager.jl with idle-sweep support and atomic transition helpers for terminal-state races. Done when idle timeout and lifecycle race scenarios are covered by tests.
notes:
Files: src/session/manager.jl
Tests first: idle timeout and lifecycle race scenarios
Verify: just test

title: Add explicit session lifecycle state and eval task tracking
description: Extend src/session/module_session.jl with lifecycle state, eval task references, and last-activity tracking. Why: interrupt, timeout, and idle cleanup need explicit state rather than implicit behavior. Done when unit tests cover lifecycle transitions and eval_task assignment timing.
notes:
Files: src/session/module_session.jl
Tests first: lifecycle transitions and eval_task assignment timing
Verify: just test

title: Add per-session FIFO serialization and activity/history updates
description: Route eval through a per-session serialization primitive and update activity/history bookkeeping in src/middleware/core.jl. Done when same-session FIFO ordering and cross-session independence are covered by tests.
notes:
Files: src/middleware/core.jl
Tests first: same-session FIFO ordering and cross-session independence
Verify: just test

title: Wire julia_new_session, julia_list_sessions, and julia_close_session to lifecycle ops
description: Extend the adapter-facing helper/client abstraction so session lifecycle tools map cleanly onto server clone/list/close operations once default-session behavior is in place. Why: Phase 2 is complete only when the adapter can use the new lifecycle operations consistently. Done when tests show these tool flows can be wired to server lifecycle ops without introducing a full adapter runtime.
notes:
Files: src/mcp_adapter.jl
Tests first: test/unit/mcp_adapter_test.jl
Verify: just test

title: Implement clone, close, and ls-sessions middleware
description: Add src/middleware/clone.jl, src/middleware/close.jl, and src/middleware/ls_sessions.jl with Phase 1 scope limited to empty-session creation plus parent-session existence validation. Why: named session lifecycle must be observable through protocol operations before adapter work proceeds. Done when clone/close/ls-sessions integration tests pass and just test is green.
notes:
Files: src/middleware/clone.jl, src/middleware/close.jl, src/middleware/ls_sessions.jl
Suggested sequence: failing tests for clone first, then close, then ls-sessions.
Verify: just test

title: Integrate named-session eval persistence and end-to-end coverage
description: Update src/middleware/core.jl so eval against a named session reuses the persistent module rather than creating ephemeral state, and add integration/e2e coverage for repeated-session bindings. Why: this is the user-visible proof that named sessions work. Done when repeated eval requests against one session persist bindings and just test is green.
notes:
Files: src/middleware/core.jl
Tests first: integration + e2e coverage for repeated named-session evals.
Verify: just test

title: Refactor session registry for persistent named session identity
description: Refactor src/session/module_session.jl and src/session/manager.jl so persistent named sessions have explicit identity and metadata while ephemeral sessions remain separately tracked. Why: later lifecycle and adapter work depends on a clean separation between named and ephemeral sessions. Done when unit tests cover create/list/lookup/destroy behavior, metadata/state basics, and the invariant that ephemeral sessions never appear in ls-sessions output.
notes:
Tests first: test/unit/session_registry_test.jl and test/integration/session_lifecycle_test.jl.
Verify: just test
Invariant: ephemeral sessions are tracked separately from persistent named sessions, MUST never appear in ls-sessions, and persistent ids refer only to named sessions.

title: Add named-session request routing and session-not-found handling
description: Update src/middleware/session.jl so session-bearing requests resolve named sessions, sessionless eval/load paths keep ephemeral behavior, and missing named sessions return session-not-found. Why: protocol correctness and lifecycle middleware depend on consistent routing semantics. Done when integration tests cover named-session routing, fallback ephemeral routing, and session-not-found responses.
notes:
Files: src/middleware/session.jl
Tests first: test/integration/session_lifecycle_test.jl
Verify: just test

title: Phase 5 — Session semantics and lifecycle correctness
description: Implement plan Phase 5. Add explicit session lifecycle state, FIFO eval serialization, task tracking, activity updates, and idle sweep semantics so session behavior matches the spec and supports later interrupt/timeout work. Done when same-session FIFO behavior, cross-session independence, idle timeout, and race handling are covered by tests.
notes:
Plan: .wai/projects/tracer-bullet/plans/2026-04-19-spec-gap-implementation-plan.md
Specs: openspec/specs/session-management/spec.md

title: Phase 2 — MCP adapter default-session lifecycle
description: Implement plan Phase 2. Extend the helper/client abstraction in src/mcp_adapter.jl so omitted-session calls use an adapter-owned persistent default session without introducing a full stdio-hosted runtime. Done when adapter-facing tests show persistent default-session behavior and session lifecycle tools can be wired to server ops.
notes:
Plan: .wai/projects/tracer-bullet/plans/2026-04-19-spec-gap-implementation-plan.md
Specs: openspec/specs/mcp-adapter/spec.md, openspec/specs/session-management/spec.md

title: Phase 1 — Persistent named sessions and lifecycle ops
description: Implement the foundational session refactor from .wai/projects/tracer-bullet/plans/2026-04-19-spec-gap-implementation-plan.md Phase 1. This epic establishes persistent named sessions alongside ephemeral eval sessions, with explicit separation invariants, so later adapter and core-op work can build on a stable session model. Done when clone/close/ls-sessions and named-session eval persistence are covered by tests and just test is green.
notes:
Plan: .wai/projects/tracer-bullet/plans/2026-04-19-spec-gap-implementation-plan.md
Specs: openspec/specs/session-management/spec.md, openspec/specs/core-operations/spec.md

title: Implement named session lifecycle for MCP adapter
description:
Implement the minimum server-side named-session support needed for Phase 2 MCP adapter work.

Why this issue exists:
- the adapter needs a persistent default session to satisfy REQ-RPL-074
- current REPLy only supports ephemeral eval sessions
- Phase 2 depends on named session lifecycle operations being available through the server

Done when:
- omitted-session adapter calls can target a persistent default session across calls
- adapter-facing helper/client flows can rely on named-session lifecycle operations for new/list/close behavior
- this remains scoped to helper/client behavior inside this repo, not a full stdio-hosted adapter runtime
notes:
Plan: .wai/projects/tracer-bullet/plans/2026-04-19-spec-gap-implementation-plan.md Phase 2
Specs: openspec/specs/mcp-adapter/spec.md (REQ-RPL-074), openspec/specs/session-management/spec.md
Verify: just test

title: Implement MCP Adapter reference client
notes: 2026-04-19: landed MCP adapter reference-client primitives in src/mcp_adapter.jl with unit coverage. Added initialize payload, eight-tool catalog, julia_eval request shaping (allow-stdin:false + ephemeral sentinel), Reply stream collection, and Reply→MCP CallToolResult mapping. Remaining adapter transport/default-session work is now blocked by REPLy_jl-ulg for persistent named sessions.

title: Implement Unix Domain Socket transport
Clarify and harden malformed JSON behavior at the TCP boundary
description:
## Context
The malformed-JSON contract is currently ambiguous between `.wai/projects/tracer-bullet/research/2026-04-17-tracer-bullet-scope-a-tracer-bullet-cuts-throu.md` and `.wai/projects/tracer-bullet/designs/2026-04-17-testing-validation-strategy-principle-o.md`, while the implementation currently causes the transport to return nothing and the connection to end quietly.

This issue is intentionally independent of the eval-streaming work because it concerns the TCP boundary before normal handler execution.

## Files
- src/protocol/message.jl
- src/transport/tcp.jl
- test/unit/message_test.jl
- test/e2e/
- .wai/projects/tracer-bullet/research/2026-04-17-tracer-bullet-scope-a-tracer-bullet-cuts-throu.md
- .wai/projects/tracer-bullet/designs/2026-04-17-testing-validation-strategy-principle-o.md

## Done
- [ ] The project chooses one explicit contract for malformed wire JSON: structured protocol error response or intentional connection close.
- [ ] The chosen contract is recorded in issue notes before implementation changes land.
- [ ] Unit/e2e tests cover the chosen behavior.
- [ ] Code and tracer-bullet docs agree on the behavior.
- [ ] `just --justfile justfile test` passes.

## Workflow Mandate
- Resolve the contract before changing implementation.
- Record the chosen malformed-JSON contract in issue notes first, then add failing tests, then implement, then align docs.
- Keep scope focused on malformed JSON at the TCP boundary only.
notes: Chosen malformed-JSON contract: treat malformed wire JSON as a closed transport boundary. The server sends no structured protocol error because request parsing never yields a valid id envelope; it closes the connection quietly after the malformed line. This preserves the existing receive(::JSONTransport) -> nothing contract and keeps malformed framing behavior aligned with EOF/partial-read handling under REQ-RPL-040b.

title: Clean up GitHub Actions and run tests in pre-push hook
description:
Why: streamline CI automation and ensure local pushes are gated by the Julia test suite.

What needs to be done:
- review existing GitHub Actions workflows and simplify where appropriate
- update local hook configuration so pre-push runs the test suite
- adjust docs or task runner commands if needed
- validate the updated workflows and hooks

title: Tidy tracer-bullet implementation after green suite
description:
## Context
Plan section: TDD progression step 13.

Perform the post-green cleanup pass once the tracer-bullet tests are passing. This is the dedicated Tidy First follow-up for small structural improvements that became obvious during implementation.

Before starting, record the intended tidy goals in issue notes so this stays a bounded cleanup pass rather than an open-ended refactor bucket.

## Files
- `src/`
- `test/`
- `README.md`
- `justfile`

## Done
- [ ] Cleanup/refactor changes are behavior-preserving and backed by the passing tracer-bullet suite.
- [ ] Naming/module boundaries are improved only for the specific friction points identified during implementation.
- [ ] `README.md` is updated only if tracer-bullet developer usage or workflow changed.
- [ ] `justfile` is updated only if a stable new command or workflow is required by the tracer bullet.
- [ ] `just test` passes after the tidy pass.

## Workflow Mandate
- Do not mix this issue with the feature-green path; start only after the tracer-bullet suite is green.
- Keep refactors small, explicit, and behavior-preserving.
- Record noteworthy design lessons in issue notes and/or `wai` artifacts.
notes: Completed bounded tidy pass after the green tracer-bullet suite: introduced a small server_port accessor instead of test-only field peeking, centralized closed-connection task handling helpers, and removed finished client tasks from the server handle during teardown. Behavior is unchanged; just test stays green.

title: Scaffold minimal Julia package for REPLy.jl
description: Create a minimal Julia package scaffold for REPLy.jl with , , and  so repository automation has a real package to exercise. Keep it intentionally small and compatible with the existing just/test/coverage setup.

title: Add e2e coverage for listener-global sessions and limits

title: Add multi-listener server orchestration
description: Extend src/server.jl so multiple listeners can run concurrently while sharing one session and limit domain. Done when concurrent listener orchestration is covered by tests.

title: Add built-in middleware descriptors and spec-compliant default stack order
description: Add descriptor methods to built-in middleware and bring the default middleware stack into spec-compliant order. Done when descriptor content and default stack composition tests pass.

title: Drive describe output from descriptor metadata where appropriate
description: Upgrade describe from a static ops catalog to descriptor-backed output after middleware metadata is stable. Done when describe output remains correct using descriptor data.

title: Implement graceful shutdown ordering
description: Add orderly shutdown sequencing on top of shared state and cleanup hooks so active work is interrupted and listeners/connections close cleanly. Done when shutdown scenarios are covered by integration/e2e tests.

title: Implement runtime timeout cancellation
description: Add runtime timeout enforcement in eval/server coordination, separate from the request validation already planned in Phase 6. Done when timeout cancellation behavior is covered by tests.

title: Implement disconnect cleanup and closed-channel response resilience
description: Ensure disconnects interrupt or clean up in-flight work appropriately and that closed response paths discard safely. Done when disconnect cleanup and post-disconnect output discard scenarios are covered by tests.

title: Enforce rate limits and oversized-message handling
description: Add rate-limit enforcement and oversized-message close behavior in transport/server paths using shared state. Done when rate-limit and oversized-message scenarios are covered by tests.

title: Introduce shared server state for limits, audit, and session coordination

title: Enforce session-count and concurrency limits

title: Ignore local external specification artifact
description: Add a gitignore rule for the untracked local file  so it does not keep appearing as workspace noise. Do not delete user data; just ignore it locally.

value: Pipeline steps (implement, review, fix, commit) must run inside subagents to preserve main conversation context. Only short summaries return to the main conversation.
